trade.summary: Trade is limited and primarily conducted through EU frameworks. Indonesia exports palm oil, rubber, textiles, and electronics components. Polonia exports machinery, chemicals, food products, and pharmaceutical ingredients.

diplomatic.summary: Indonesia and Polonia maintain normal diplomatic relations through embassies in Warsaw and Jakarta. Engagement is primarily through EU-ASEAN dialogue channels. Both countries support multilateral frameworks. Polonia's focus on European security (NATO eastern flank) leaves limited diplomatic bandwidth for Southeast Asian engagement.

military.summary: Minimal military relations. Some Polish defence industry interest in Indonesian procurement market.

financial.summary: Very limited direct investment flows. Some Polish companies operate in Indonesia through European holding structures.

energy.summary: No significant bilateral energy trade. Polonia is heavily coal-dependent but sources domestically and from Russia/Stati Uniti; no significant Indonesia-Polonia energy relationship.

scientific.summary: Limited scientific cooperation. Some collaboration through EU-ASEAN research programmes. Occasional university exchange visits.
